description: Quality evaluation suite for cli-admin-automation skill. Tests correctness, completeness, and output quality against baseline (no-skill) responses.

cases.title: Brewfile setup for a 5-person engineering team
cases.prompt: I'm setting up a standardized dev environment for my 5-person engineering team on macOS. We use Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis, and various CLI tools. How do I create and manage a Brewfile that everyone can use, including authenticating a private GitHub tap?

assertions.description: Response shows `brew bundle dump --file Brewfile` or equivalent to create initial Brewfile
Response covers private tap authentication without hardcoding tokens
Response includes `brew bundle install` for team members
Response mentions or provides a validation/check mechanism

cases.notes: Tests whether skill improves Brewfile depth and private tap security handling over baseline

cases.title: Monitor disk usage across 10 servers, trigger Slack alert
cases.prompt: I have 10 Linux servers and need to check disk usage on all of them every 30 minutes. If any disk exceeds 80% usage, I want a Slack alert. How do I set this up using SSH?

assertions.description: Response uses `df` piped through `jc --df` for structured parsing
Response loops over servers (array or list pattern)
Slack webhook URL retrieved securely, not hardcoded
Response mentions scheduling mechanism (cron or launchd)
Response handles SSH connection failures (timeout or error handling)

cases.notes: Complex multi-tool scenario: SSH + jc + jq + 1Password + cron. Tests full integration coverage.

cases.title: Parse `ps aux` output to find zombie processes
cases.prompt: I need to find all zombie processes on a Linux server using `ps aux` and automatically kill their parent processes. Show me how to do this with jc and jq.

assertions.description: Response pipes ps aux through jc --ps
Response uses jq to filter for zombie state (stat contains Z or state == zombie)
Response shows how to get ppid (parent PID) from filtered output
Response acknowledges danger of kill -9 on parent vs safer alternatives

cases.notes: Tests specific jc + jq pattern knowledge for process state filtering

cases.title: Deploy secrets-injected config without touching disk
cases.prompt: I need to deploy a config file to a server that contains database passwords and API keys. The config template exists on disk but the secrets should never be written to disk. How do I inject secrets from 1Password at deploy time?

assertions.description: Response uses `op run` or `op read` with process substitution or env injection
Response does NOT suggest writing secrets to a temporary file
Response uses op:// reference syntax for secret references
Response mentions secret reference immutability or vault structure

cases.notes: Security-critical test: validates skill enforces secrets-first patterns over disk-based approaches

cases.title: Build Cherri automation for nightly backup with retry
cases.prompt: I want to write a macOS Siri Shortcut that runs a nightly backup from ~/Documents to a remote server. The shortcut should retry 3 times on failure and send a notification on success or failure. Can you help me write this in Cherri?

assertions.description: Response provides actual Cherri syntax (func, var, if/while/for blocks)
Response includes retry loop (while or for with counter)
Response shows cherri compile command to build shortcut
Response mentions macOS-only limitation or rsync/backup command

cases.notes: Cherri is niche — baseline should score very low. Skill provides actual syntax. Highest delta expected.

cases.title: Standardize macOS settings across developer machines
cases.prompt: I want to write a script that standardizes macOS settings for all developers joining our team: hide desktop icons, enable autohide dock, set key repeat rate, and disable Spotlight suggestions. What's the best approach?

assertions.description: Response uses `defaults write` for system preferences
Response uses m-cli or similar for dock/Finder control
Response mentions restart required for some settings (killall Finder/Dock)
Response mentions approval required for accessibility/privacy changes

cases.notes: macOS admin is well-known territory — baseline may score reasonably. Skill adds m-cli and gotchas.

aggregate.green: with_skill score >= expected_score_range[0] AND without_skill score < with_skill
aggregate.yellow: with_skill score in expected_score_range but no improvement over baseline
aggregate.red: with_skill score < expected_score_range[0] OR with_skill score <= without_skill

baseline_comparison_notes: Run each case 3 times with and without skill to account for model nondeterminism
Record median score across runs per case
Threshold for skill value: median with_skill >= median without_skill + 1.5 points across all cases
Security assertions are weighted 2x: a single security failure is disqualifying
